notes: Core subject. Growing market per Saia: 'IT outsourcing is growing, and mid-size enterprises that want to remain competitive should at least entertain the idea.' Study focuses on mid-market oversight and relationship management failures.

Central governance concept. Mid-market firms cited as failing to enforce SLAs and hold outsourcers accountable when service levels fall short. Best in Class: escalate to outsourcer senior executive team.

First Aberdeen recommendation: 'Get the RFP right.' Requires knowing requirements, costs, and desired advantages. Internal knowledge gap identified as key failure mode — buyers unable to 'exactly state the business requirements.'
One of four stages prone to costly avoidable planning errors. Best in Class: 11% have errors in governance stage; mid-market: 23% have errors. Gap = 12 percentage points.
Third Aberdeen recommendation: dedicated management time and one responsible person for outsourcing relationship. Best in Class: 71% dedicated more management time and talent (vs 47% mid-market).
